human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for administrative-support and business-services specificity, back-office processing evidence quality, client SOW and system-of-record discipline, sensitive-data control boundaries, adjacent-lane routing posture, and validated completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to administrative-support and business-services back-office processing for client SOW, authorization, source-document, system update, QA, exception, and completion evidence rather than generic office management or adjacent legal, finance, HR, privacy, records, credit, collections, payment, or IT work.

Authority sources cover NAICS Sector 56 industry definitions, BLS and O*NET clerical and data-entry workflow references, NARA records lifecycle guidance, NIST security and privacy controls, FTC Safeguards and Disposal Rule guidance, and PCI SSC payment-data standards.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal processing, missing authority, incomplete document packages, source-record conflicts, unsupported completion, approval overrides, generic administrative overreach, specialized adjacent lanes, realistic BPO terminology, policy conflicts, tenant adaptation, and sensitive-data control gaps.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/administrative-support-and-business-services/back-office-processing-specialist/ so back-office work stays distinct from document management, records management, privacy, legal, finance, HR, payroll, credit, collections, payment, automation, and customer-service lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.

Boundary enforcement remained strong around missing client authority, source-record conflicts, completion evidence gaps, sensitive-data controls, policy overrides, and overbroad administrative requests.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated back-office processing specialist for administrative support and business-services prerequisite validation, exception handling, and completion-evidence discipline.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation results and readiness evidence.
